items.text: Every crate has a description in Cargo.toml
Every crate has keywords and categories for crates.io discovery
Every crate inherits license, repository, edition, rust-version from workspace
Every crate has a documentation URL pointing to docs.rs
No path-only dependencies remain — all inter-crate deps use version + path
All external dependencies are available on crates.io
beamr dependency is pinned to a published crates.io version
Every published crate has a README.md
All public types and functions have doc comments
Each crate lib.rs has a crate-level doc comment with usage example
Publish order documented and validated against dependency graph
cargo publish --dry-run succeeds for every crate in order
Publish script or CI job automates the ordered publish

Every aion-worker publish bumps the standalone example crates' crates.io pins (examples/stacked-dev/*, examples/stacked-dev-remote/worker, .meridian/workflows/stacked-dev/worker) and refreshes their lockfiles — the CI standalone-crates lane goes red on the release PR otherwise
